text: Neurology appointment. Dr. Okafor showed me the tremor progression chart. It's a slow line going up. He said 'slow is good.' I keep staring at the direction.
Sofia asked me to braid her hair tonight. My hands couldn't do it. Elena took over without missing a beat. Sofia didn't notice. I did.
PT session. Gait and balance work. The therapist filmed my walk. It looks almost normal on camera. That word — almost.
First LSVT LOUD session. The speech therapist made me shout vowels for an hour. My voice was louder leaving than arriving. I didn't realize how quiet I'd gotten.
Valentine's Day. Elena and I stayed in. Sofia made us paper hearts. I tried to cut one out with scissors but my hand shook too much. She said 'it's okay Daddy, yours is special because it's wiggly.'
First Rock Steady Boxing class. Everyone in the room has Parkinson's. For the first time since diagnosis I wasn't the only one shaking. That felt like something.
Second LSVT session. We practiced projecting voice across the room. The therapist said I need to think I'm shouting when I'm actually just at normal volume. My calibration is off.
PT session. Upper limb coordination drills. Stacking blocks, turning pages, buttoning a shirt. I used to design interfaces. Now I practice buttoning.
Couldn't sleep. My right arm was rigid and my leg was restless. Elena was asleep next to me breathing slowly. I didn't want to wake her. She carries enough.
Work standup call. Gave my project update by voice. Nobody asked me to type anything. My manager set it up that way on purpose. I haven't thanked her properly.
Boxing class. Worked the heavy bag for three rounds straight. My arms were jelly after. My head was clear for the first time all week.
Played catch with Sofia in the backyard. Dropped the ball three times. She ran and picked it up every time without comment. Seven years old and already more graceful than me.
PT session. Balance test showed improvement from last month. The therapist said boxing is helping. Nice when two things reinforce each other.
Support group at Dell Medical. Met a guy named Chris, 42, diagnosed five years ago. He still runs his landscaping business. He said the first year is the scariest. I'm in it.
Standup call at work. Someone asked me to drop notes in Slack after. I dictated them using Win+H. It worked. Not perfect but readable.
Boxing class. Coach taught us defensive slips today. It's weird — I dodge punches better than I open jars.
Morning medication kicked in and I had two solid hours of steady hands. Typed a whole email to my mom without voice-to-text. It felt like driving a car I used to own.
Pharmacy pickup. Pramipexole and rasagiline. $62 copay. The pharmacist asked if I wanted easy-open caps. I said yes without hesitating. No ego left about that.
OT session. Amy showed me adaptive tech — large trackball mouse, voice control, a thick-grip stylus for the tablet. I used to mock my dad for his reading glasses. Karma.
Work standup. Gave a clean update on the Meridian project. My voice was strong — LSVT exercises are working. Manager said 'loud and clear.' She doesn't know how literal that is.
Boxing. Sparred light rounds with another member named Dave. He's 55 and hits harder than me. We fist-bumped after with shaking gloves.
Elena made dinner tonight. She does that every night now. I used to cook for the family three times a week. I miss the sound of my own chopping.
PT session. Gait work. I walked heel-to-toe down a line for ten meters without stepping off. Small win but the therapist cheered like it was the Super Bowl.
Sofia's talent show tonight. She sang 'You Are My Sunshine.' She looked right at me during the chorus. I held it together until we got to the car.
Work standup. Good call. The team is used to my voice-only setup now. Nobody asks why I don't share my screen anymore.
Support group. Topic was relationships after diagnosis. A woman said her husband left. A man said his wife became his nurse. I thought about Elena. She's become both more and I hate that.
PT upper limb session. Tried to thread a needle as a dexterity test. Couldn't do it. The therapist said that's not the goal — the goal is function, not perfection.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Okafor says tremor is stable. Medication working. He used the word 'plateau.' I've never been so happy to hear a word that means nothing's changing.
Boxing class. Hit the speed bag for the first time since diagnosis. Messy but rhythmic. It's like typing used to be — muscle memory under pressure.
Elena fell asleep on the couch grading papers. She teaches all day and comes home to a second shift of managing everything I can't. I covered her with a blanket. That's all I could do.
PT session. Balance test scores are the best since I started. The therapist printed the chart. It's on my fridge now next to Sofia's drawings.
OT session. Amy set up Dragon NaturallySpeaking on my laptop. Dictated a full project memo in ten minutes. It's not typing but it's close enough.
Standup call. Used Dragon to take notes during the meeting and dropped them into Slack immediately after. Fastest turnaround I've had in months.
Boxing. Two months in. I can do the full warmup, three rounds on the bag, and the cooldown without sitting. My stamina is back to where it was pre-diagnosis.
Support group. A neurologist from UT presented on exercise and neuroplasticity. She said boxing literally builds new neural pathways. I punched harder at my next class.
PT stretching and rigidity session. My right arm is tighter in the afternoons. The therapist gave me a foam roller routine for evenings.
Sofia's end-of-year recital. She played recorder with her class. Missed a few notes and grinned the whole time. I recorded it on my phone — shaky video, perfect memory.
Work standup. Gave a good update. My manager said 'Daniel, your project thinking is as sharp as ever.' I needed that today.
Boxing class. Coach introduced pad work in pairs. My partner's name is Marcus, 47. He said 'nice jab.' We don't talk about why we're here.
Tried to assemble a bookshelf for Sofia's room. Got halfway through before the screwdriver kept slipping. Elena finished it. She said 'teamwork.' It didn't feel like teamwork.
Pharmacy pickup. Pramipexole refill. $38. The pharmacist knows my name now. She puts the cap on loosely without me asking.
Work standup. Announced I finished the Meridian documentation entirely by voice dictation. Nobody knew. That's the whole point.
Boxing class. Coach said my right cross is getting faster. I told him it's the only thing about my right hand that's getting better. He laughed. I meant it.
Annual checkup with Dr. Mendez. Blood pressure normal, weight stable. She said 'you're in better shape than most 38-year-olds I see.' She doesn't count the tremor.
PT gait session. The therapist added tandem walking — heel touching toe. I wobbled but didn't fall. She spotted me the whole time like a gymnast.
Support group. Topic was workplace disclosure. Chris said he told his crew and they adapted. I still haven't told my whole team. Only my manager knows.
Standup call. Afternoon — bad timing. My voice was softer because morning LSVT wears off. Manager asked me to repeat one point. I projected and got it out.
Boxing. Last class before summer schedule shift. Hit 200 punches in three rounds. My arms were noodles and my brain was quiet. That's the trade.
PT upper limb session. Practiced typing on a large-key keyboard. Got 22 WPM. Used to do 90. The therapist said 22 is functional. I said 22 is humbling.
Barton Springs with Elena and Sofia. Floated in the cold water for an hour. No tremor in the water — my body doesn't shake when it's buoyant. I just floated and forgot.
Work standup. Short call. Gave my update in two minutes flat. Manager said 'efficient as always.' She doesn't know it's because talking gets harder as the day goes on.
Boxing. Summer heat makes everything harder. Drank a liter of water during class. My punches were slower but I finished every round.

Sofia asked if I'm sick. I said 'not exactly.' She said 'then why do your hands do that?' Elena looked at me. I told Sofia about Parkinson's. Simple words. She said 'okay' and went back to drawing. Kids.
PT gait and balance. Best session yet. I walked backwards down the hallway without losing balance. The therapist said my boxing is showing.
Neurology follow-up. Discussed medication adjustment. Pramipexole dose going up slightly. Dr. Okafor said the wearing-off I notice in the afternoon is expected at this stage.
Work standup. Mentioned I've been using voice dictation for all documentation. A colleague asked what tool I use. He wants to try it for himself. That felt good.
Boxing. Coach paired me with a new member — a woman named Lisa, 39, just diagnosed. She looked like I looked four months ago. I said 'it gets easier to be here.'
Support group. Medication wearing-off topic. A pharmacist explained how dopamine levels dip. Seeing it on a graph made my afternoons make sense for the first time.
PT stretching session. The therapist worked on my right shoulder rigidity. It's worse in the afternoon heat. She said cold packs help. I'll try it.
OT session. Amy set up my phone with bigger touch targets and longer hold delays. The accidental taps are gone. I can text Elena without sending garbled messages.
Boxing. Five months in. Coach had us do timed combinations. I got through the set faster than last month. The stopwatch doesn't lie.
Sunday afternoon. Sofia wanted to play a video game together. I couldn't press the buttons fast enough. She switched to a game with simpler controls without saying why. She's seven going on forty.
Work standup. Manager asked how the voice-to-text workflow is going. Told her it's working well. She's been great about all of this. Not every boss would be.
PT gait session. The therapist added dual-task walking — walk while counting backwards. My brain and legs argued. But I stayed upright.
Couldn't open a water bottle. Sat on the couch holding it for a full minute before Elena noticed and opened it. She handed it back like nothing happened. I drank it and felt small.
Pharmacy pickup. Both meds ready. $62. The HEB pharmacist put them in a bag with handles because she noticed I was having a shaky day.
Support group. Assistive tech demos. Someone showed an adaptive gaming controller. I thought about Sofia's video games. Ordered one that night.
Dropped Sofia off for her first day of 2nd grade. She held my hand walking in. I held hers back as steady as I could. She waved from the classroom door.
PT upper limb session. Practiced fastening buttons. Got five out of eight. The therapist said three months ago I got two. I'm choosing to focus on that.
Elena and I talked after Sofia went to bed. She said she's tired but she's not going anywhere. I said I know. She said 'no, I mean it.' I needed to hear the emphasis.
Work standup. First one after summer break. The team has a new member. She asked why I do voice-only. Manager said 'Daniel prefers it.' Clean and simple.
Boxing. Back on the regular schedule. Punched bags for an hour. Sweat through my shirt. Left feeling more like myself than I have in weeks.
Tried to write Sofia a note for her lunchbox like Elena does. My handwriting was illegible. I typed it in big font and printed it. It said 'Dad loves you. Have a great day.'
PT gait session. Walked a full lap around the clinic hallway without touching the wall. Six months ago I needed the rail. The numbers are real.
Support group. Topic was finances and disability. Learned about SSDI. Didn't think I was 'disabled enough.' The facilitator said that's the most common mistake young-onset people make.
Work standup. Told the team about a process improvement idea I had. They liked it. For twenty minutes I was just a project coordinator with good ideas, not a guy with Parkinson's.
Boxing class. Lisa the new member is getting stronger. She thanked me for being welcoming that first day. I remember being welcomed too.
Afternoon wearing-off hit hard today. Tremor, stiffness, everything. Elena found me sitting on the bedroom floor. Not fallen — just sitting. Sometimes I need to be low.
PT stretching session. The foam roller routine is helping with evening rigidity. I do it while Sofia watches cartoons. She calls it 'Dad's floor time.'
Good morning. Hands steady until 11am. Made pancakes for Sofia. She put a smiley face on mine with blueberries. I ate around it to keep the face intact as long as possible.
Work standup. Quick call. I'm getting good at saying what matters in two minutes. Parkinson's has made me a better communicator by forcing me to be concise.
Boxing. Worked on footwork and balance drills. Coach says I move better than when I started. The improvement is slow but the line goes in the right direction.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Okafor did a tremor reassessment. Slight progression in the right hand. He said 'slight' twice like he wanted me to hear that word specifically.
PT gait session. The therapist added obstacle navigation — stepping over foam blocks while walking. I knocked two over. She said 'progress is messy.'
OT session. Amy helped me set up Alexa for medication reminders and hands-free calling. I said 'Alexa, call Elena' and it just worked. Technology being what it's supposed to be.
My old coworker from the design team texted asking if I want to grab coffee. I said sure. Didn't tell him about the Parkinson's. I just want one conversation where I'm the old me.
Work standup. Routine. Good. I like routine now. It used to bore me. Now it means the system is holding.
Support group. Fall prevention topic. The physical therapist showed bathroom modification photos. Elena and I should do that. I keep saying 'should' instead of 'will.'
PT upper limb coordination. Practiced pouring water from a pitcher. Got it in the glass every time. Three months ago I splashed the table.
Halloween. Sofia was a dinosaur. Elena was a paleontologist. I wore boxing gloves and a robe. We trick-or-treated for two hours. My legs were done but Sofia wasn't. I kept going.
Boxing class. My legs were sore from last night's walking but I showed up. Coach said showing up is 80% of this. I believe him.
Year-end review coming up at work. My manager sent a heads-up email. She wants to talk about 'adjustments for next year.' That could mean anything. I'm choosing to trust her.
Work standup. Gave a clear update. My voice was strong this morning. The LSVT exercises pay off when I'm consistent with them.
PT gait session. Did the obstacle course without knocking anything over. The therapist said 'that's a first.' I said 'let me do it again to prove it wasn't a fluke.' It wasn't.
Pharmacy pickup. Pramipexole refill. $38. I set up auto-refill today. One less thing to remember, one less trip to manage.
Elena and I looked at our finances tonight. The credit card is slowly going down. Medical costs are steady. She said 'we're okay.' I said 'we're managing.' Same thing, different feeling.
Support group. Holiday energy management. Chris said he plans rest days around family events now. No guilt. Just math. I'm going to try that for Thanksgiving.
Work standup. Last one before Thanksgiving. Everyone shared what they're grateful for. I said 'voice dictation.' They laughed. I wasn't entirely joking.
PT stretching session. The therapist worked on my right shoulder and arm. Cold weather is making the rigidity worse. She gave me warm-up exercises for mornings.
Thanksgiving. Elena's parents came. I set the table with Sofia. Dropped a fork twice. Her grandpa picked it up both times and said 'butterfingers run in families.' He doesn't know. Or maybe he does.
December. One year since I told Elena about the diagnosis. She cried that night. I held her with shaking hands. We're both different people now.
Work standup. Manager mentioned my year-end review will be in two weeks. She said 'nothing to worry about.' I'm trying to believe that.
Boxing class. December session started. Only six of us showed up. Coach said the holidays thin the ranks. We hit harder to make up for it.
OT session. Amy reviewed my winter toolkit — heated gloves, weighted utensils for stiff mornings, warm-up stretches before leaving the house. Cold is my enemy now.
PT gait session. Did well despite the cold. Balance holding steady. The therapist said I can reduce to monthly PT in the new year if I keep up home exercises.
Sofia's holiday concert. She sang in the front row. Looked right at me and Elena. I held Elena's hand with my left hand — steady one — and recorded with my right — shaky one. Got the whole song.
Support group holiday gathering. Potluck. Chris brought chili, Lisa brought cornbread. We ate and talked about next year's goals. Mine is to still be boxing in December 2025.
Christmas morning. Sofia opened presents. I helped her with the packaging — she ripped, I held. Elena took a photo of us that I'll keep forever. My hands are blurry in it. Everything else is sharp.
Elena left a note in my stocking. It said 'You're the strongest person I know and Sofia's favorite human. Nothing changes that.' I put it in my wallet.
New Year's Eve. Elena and Sofia fell asleep at 10. I sat on the porch with a blanket and watched the fireworks from across the neighborhood. One resolution: keep showing up.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Okafor says winter rigidity is common. No medication change. 'Stay active, stay warm.' He makes it sound simple.
First standup of the new year. Team sounds refreshed. My voice was strong. Started the year the way I want to continue.
Boxing. First class of 2025. Two new faces. I introduced myself and shook their hands. Shaky handshake. They both had one too. We all laughed.
PT upper limb session. Practiced buttoning a dress shirt. Got all seven buttons in three minutes. The therapist timed me. I used to not need a timer for buttons.
Support group. New year goals topic. Chris wants to run a 5K. Lisa wants to cook for her family again. I said I want to type a full email without voice-to-text. The room went quiet. They understood.
Work standup. Normal call. I've found my rhythm — voice update, dictated notes, Slack voice message for follow-ups. The system works.
Boxing. Worked the double-end bag. It requires timing more than strength. My timing is still good. My brain compensates for what my muscles can't.
Sunday. Cooked breakfast with Sofia. She cracked the eggs because my hands were shaky. She said 'I'm your sous chef, Dad.' Elena watched from the doorway smiling.
PT gait session. Did dual-task walking again — counting backwards while navigating cones. Nailed it. The therapist said 'that's competition-level multitasking.'
Pharmacy pickup. Both meds. $62. Set up automatic payment too. The fewer things I have to manage manually, the better the system runs.
Standup call. Presented a proposal for restructuring the project tracker. Manager approved it on the call. I can still think strategically. The disease is in my hands, not my head.
Boxing. One year anniversary of my first class. Coach gave me a fist bump and said 'you're a regular now.' That word — regular. I'll take it.
OT session. Amy showed me a weighted stylus for the iPad. I drew a circle on the screen. It was wobbly but closed. She said 'that's a circle.' I said 'that's a miracle.'
PT stretching and rigidity work. Winter is almost over but my right shoulder is still tight. The therapist said spring will help. Bodies know seasons.

Valentine's Day. Made a card for Elena using large print from the computer. Sofia helped me glue a heart on it. Elena cried when she read it. She said 'you used the computer.' I said 'I used my voice.'
Support group. SSDI topic. The legal navigator walked us through the application. I took notes by voice memo. It's a mountain of paperwork. Elena said she'll help.
Work standup. Mentioned I'm exploring SSDI for the future. Manager said 'we'll support whatever you need.' I believe her.
Boxing. Sparred with Marcus. He caught me with a jab I didn't see coming. I slipped the next three. Parkinson's hasn't taken my reflexes yet.
Sunday morning. Sat on the porch with coffee. My hands were steady — morning meds at full strength. A cardinal sat on the fence. We looked at each other. Quiet mornings are medicine too.
PT gait session. Best balance score of the year so far. The therapist said boxing and PT together are keeping me ahead of the curve. Ahead. I like that word.
Sofia drew a picture of our family. In it, my hands are big and strong. That's how she sees me. I put it on the fridge next to the PT chart.
Work standup. Clean update. I've been at this part-time role for over a year now. It works. It's not who I was but it's who I am and that's enough.
Boxing. Coach put on music during the workout today. Punching to the beat is easier. Rhythm helps tremor. I didn't know that until I felt it.
Speech therapy maintenance session. The therapist said my volume has held well since the LSVT block last year. Keep doing daily exercises. Five minutes. Every morning.
PT upper limb session. Practiced writing my name. Legible on the first try. The therapist took a photo. I don't know if she keeps a gallery of handwriting wins but she should.
Support group. Exercise updates — boxing, cycling, dance. A woman started PD dance classes and said it changed her life. I might try it. Elena would come with me.
Work standup. Routine call. I'm reliable. That matters more to me now than being brilliant. Reliable is underrated.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Okafor said tremor is stable. Discussed adding a small afternoon booster dose to bridge the wearing-off gap. I said yes. Anything to keep the afternoons functional.
PT gait session. Did a timed walk test. Fastest time since I started. The therapist high-fived me. I caught her hand on the first try.
Spring. Windows open. Sofia riding her bike in the driveway. Elena grading papers on the porch. Me watching from the steps with my coffee. This is the life I'm fighting to keep.
Standup call. New quarter kickoff. I'm leading a workstream for the first time since going part-time. Manager said 'you're ready.' I think she's right.
Boxing. Spring session. Coach added a balance beam drill. I walked it end to end without stepping off. Dave clapped. I took a bow.
OT session. Amy showed me button-hook tools and elastic shoelaces. I tried them. Getting dressed took half the time. I stared at my shoes and thought 'this is freedom.'
PT stretching session. Right arm rigidity better since the weather warmed up. The therapist said I can stretch the PT visits to every three weeks if summer goes well.
Sofia's soccer game. She scored a goal and ran to me on the sideline. I picked her up — both arms, no shake, pure adrenaline. Elena got it on video. I've watched it eleven times.

Work standup. Led the workstream update. Clear, concise, confident. A colleague messaged me after: 'great presentation.' She has no idea how much that costs me in energy and how much it gives me back.
Support group. A new member, 36, just diagnosed. He sat in the back and barely spoke. I walked over after and said 'the first one is the hardest. Come back next month.' He nodded.

PT gait session. Did the full circuit — tandem walk, obstacle course, dual-task counting. Clean run. The therapist said 'if I didn't know your diagnosis I wouldn't guess.' That's the best thing anyone's said to me in months.
Evening. Medication wearing off. Tremor rising. But the day was full — boxing Tuesday, work Wednesday, PT Thursday, Sofia's homework tonight. I'm tired and I earned it. That matters.
Sofia climbed into my lap while I was reading. She put her head on my chest and said 'your heart is loud, Daddy.' My hands were shaking but she didn't move. She just listened.
